channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Trust CEO and chair cleared by investigation
item.description: An external investigation into the conduct of a trust chair and CEO found “no case to answer” for either party, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Former Brown spin doctor to join DHSC
item.description: A former adviser to Gordon Brown is moving with Yvette Cooper from the Foreign Office to the Department of Health and Social Care, HSJ has learned.

item.title: England’s biggest ICB confirms permanent CEO
item.description: England’s largest integrated care board has appointed its interim chief executive to the role permanently.

item.title: Health Bill blocks top Burnham priority, warns Labour health expert
item.description: The government’s Health Bill is in danger of blocking integration with social care, an influential Labour MP has warned.

item.title: You’re ‘too gentle’ with those who abuse staff, police tell trust
item.description: NHS staff “tolerate too much” abuse and trusts are “too gentle” with perpetrators, police have told the workforce of a large district general hospital.

item.title: Two North West MPs made health ministers
item.description: Two new junior health ministers – both representing constituencies in the North West – have been appointed by prime minister Andy Burnham.

item.title: We must abandon the myth that inquiries work
item.description: “I don’t think people do get closure [from inquiries]… It’s a myth, and I don’t think that we should dangle the prospect of it as something that we can achieve.” Dr Bill Kirkup speaking on last week’s HSJ podcast

item.title: Watchdog warns NHSE over FDP effectiveness claims
item.description: NHS England and the Department of Health and Social Care have been told to involve their statistical teams more closely in any claims they make about the effectiveness of the federated data platform and to improve the accuracy of communications about the controversial IT system.

item.title: Corporate manslaughter investigation launched into trust
item.description: A trust that treated a man with paranoid schizophrenia who went on to kill three people is being investigated for corporate manslaughter.

item.title: More changes to DHSC ministerial team announced
item.description: An MP who called for a debate on “the revolving door of failed NHS managers” has been appointed a health minister.

item.title: Mackey must justify ‘highly irregular’ redundancy payment, demands peer
item.description: NHS England’s CEO has been challenged to explain a “highly irregular” £300,000 exit payout to a former integrated care board chief executive.

item.title: Army medics could support ‘unsafe’ service
item.description: Army medics could be brought into a service currently deemed “unsafe”, according to a struggling trust.

item.title: Trusts that stop staff criticising Israel face legal risk
item.description: Trust chief executives have been warned they could face “significant legal exposure” if they adopt a definition of antisemitism recommended by a government-commissioned review.

item.title: ICB reorganisation leads to legal battle with supplier
item.description: The cancellation of a contract awarded amid the confusion of the government’s hastily announced overhaul of integrated care boards last year has resulted in a supplier taking the NHS to the High Court.

item.title: New health secretary appointed
item.description: Yvette Cooper has been appointed secretary of state for health and social care by incoming prime minister Andy Burnham.

item.title: Make us feel ‘safe’ while we drive reform, ICBs tell NHSE
item.description: Greater “air cover” from NHS England and “assurance” that commissioning leaders will not suffer from making difficult decisions has been demanded by integrated care boards.

item.title: I was told to close down 4,000 safety reports, claims manager
item.description: A senior NHS manager told an inquiry into more than 2,000 deaths he was asked to remove thousands of outstanding safety incident reports while his trust was in the middle of the probe.

item.title: Slow down shift to neighbourhoods, says GP leader
item.description: Plans to introduce neighbourhood provider contracts next year should be “reconsidered”, the Royal College of GPs’ president has told HSJ .

item.title: First trusts win IHO status
item.description: Two trusts have become the first to win national approval to hold an integrated health organisation (IHO) contract.

item.title: Maternity ‘sidelined by boards and poorly understood by medical directors’
item.description: Maternity services, despite numerous scathing reviews, are still sidelined by trust boards and often poorly understood by medical directors, who view them as being outside the mainstream and delivered by “slightly odd folk”, a leading expert has told HSJ .
